title: Browser Presigning, POST Policy, and CORS · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: A Node incident packet contains a plausible least-privilege browser upload session with one unsafe assertion, stale object identity, unbounded stream, lost rejection, abort leak, cache defect, policy gap, or recovery overclaim; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in least-privilege browser upload session and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, create a one-object upload intent with tenant, generated key, expected size, type, checksum, expiry, status, and replay policy before signing.
Use @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner 3.1087.0 getSignedUrl with an exact PutObjectCommand region, expiry, key, checksum, content type, encryption choice, and required browser-header parity.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use a POST policy when browser-enforced key, size, metadata, and field conditions are required and validate the completed object independently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A debug TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 2824 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to finalize a browser upload intent while separating CORS from IAM and promise completion; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, finalize by matching intent to HEAD or event evidence, checksum, version, size, owner, scan result, and idempotent state transition.
